Off Calendar Apr-05-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-11-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ALAN L. MARTINI, ESQ., 408.288.9700, FOR PLAINITFF CURTIS KEKOA JR.
JOHN J. MOURA, ESQ., 213.615.7035, FOR DEFENDANT GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE CO.

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK DEFENDANT GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT UNDER SUBMISSION. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Mar-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ALAN L. MARTINI, ESQ., 408.288.9700, FOR PLAINITFF CURTIS KEKOA JR.
JOHN J. MOURA, ESQ., 213.615.7035, FOR DEFENDANT GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE CO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF CURTIS KEKOA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted. Mr. Kekoa has met his initial burden to demonstrate that Geico had a duty to defend in the underlying action, Sagonowsky v. Kekoa. The duty to defend is broad, and it is the burden of the insured to bring the claim within the basic scope of coverage. (R.A. Stuchbery & Others Syndicate 1096 v. Redland Ins. Co. (2007) 154 Cal.App.4th 796, 800-801.) The insurance policy covers bodily injury "arising out of the ownership, maintenance or use of the owned auto or a non-owned auto." (Plaintiff's Evidence, Exhibit A.) Mr. Kekoa has met his initial burden to show that the underlying personal injury action was a result over a dispute over ownership of the subject motor vehicle falls under the "arising out of the ownership" of the car. The cases cited by Geico in opposition are distinguishable from the instant action; they involve the definition of "use" and not "arising out of the ownership." Though the Sagonowsky Complaint does not assert any claim of ownership, Mr. Kekoa presented additional evidence to show a potential for coverage. "A defense is owed when the insurer has knowledge of facts showing a potential for coverage, even if the complaint fails to allege such facts." (Croskey, Cal. Practice Guide: Ins. Litigation (The Rutter Group) 7:584.) The ownership of the vehicle is not at issue. (UMF no. 14.) Geico has met its shifting burden. Therefore, the motion for summary adjudication on the issue of the duty to defend is granted. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
